channel.title: Categories of Packages
channel.description:
I'm working in the docs regarding packages and I want to explain that packages are created under a Namespace that defines the type of behaviour/feature implemented by the package and that this Namespace is also used to organize the configuration of different packages
But not all the packages are like this, we also have _core_ packages ( [`frontity`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/frontity)), _interface_ packages ( [`@frontity/source`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@frontity/source)) , _helpers_ packages ( [`@frontity/components`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@frontity/components))
How would you categorize the different types of packages?
We should find categories that allow us to assign them to each one of the [packages published in NPM](https://www.npmjs.com/search?q=frontity)

item.title: Categories of Packages
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
I’ve already applied this categorization of packages
It can be seen here → https://api.frontity.org/

Read full topic

item.title: Categories of Packages
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
This is my proposal
→ represents those packages that will be documented

Themes packages

  • twentytwenty-theme
  • mars-theme

TS Types packages

  • analytics
  • router
  • source
  • type-declarations
  • types

Core packages

  • frontity
    • babel-plugin-frontity
    • connect
    • core
    • create-frontity
    • error
    • file-settings

Helpers packages

  • components
  • hooks

Features packages

  • Analytics packages
    • comscore-analytics
    • google-analytics
    • google-tag-manager
    • google-tag-manager-analytics
  • SEO packages
    • head-tags
  • Render packages
    • html2react
  • Router packages
    • tiny-router
  • Source packages
    • wp-source

Deprecated packages

  • yoast

Read full topic

item.title: Categories of Packages
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
I’m working in the docs regarding packages and I want to explain that packages are created under a Namespace that defines the type of behaviour/feature implemented by the package and that this Namespace is also used to organize the configuration of different packages

But not all the packages are like this, we also have core packages ( frontity), interface packages ( @frontity/source) , helpers packages ( @frontity/components)

How would you categorize the different types of packages?

We should find categories that allow us to assign them to each one of the packages published in NPM

Read full topic
